title: FDA to shore up legal basis for CRL disclosures as it pauses new releases
raw_markdown:
ARTICLE \|Politics, Policy & Law

# FDA to shore up legal basis for CRL disclosures as it pauses new releases

## Proposed legal framework for disclosing complete response letters for unapproved drugs to be released in October

By Steve Usdin, Washington Editor

July 8, 2026 7:45 PM EDT

![](https://us-west-2-biocentury.graphassets.com/AqjgGpburRt67lm6SEY2Oz/cmrcqhsrb3htp07mz321kljux)

The Trump administration is pausing the release of complete response letters for unapproved drugs and moving to create the legal foundation the policy has been missing.

The Office of Management and Budget posted a [notice](https://www.reginfo.gov/public/do/eAgendaViewRule?pubId=202510&RIN=0910-AJ16) this week indicating that HHS will propose a rule allowing “proactive disclosure of complete response letters.”...

title: Race to cure HBV moves to targeting the reservoir
raw_markdown:
ARTICLE \|Product Development

# Race to cure HBV moves to targeting the reservoir

## Precision cuts HBV cccDNA while Tune and nChroma aim to shut it down with CRISPR‑based epigenetic silencers

By Danielle Golovin, Senior Biopharma Analyst

July 8, 2026 9:01 PM EDT

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

There’s now compelling evidence that eliminating viral transcripts with antisense oligonucleotides can lead to a functional HBV cure in some patients, and early clinical data from _in vivo_ gene editing and epigenetic silencing therapies suggest that directly targeting the viral DNA reservoir could take that progress a step further.

Bepirovirsen from GSK plc (LSE:GSK; NYSE:GSK) and Ionis Pharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ:IONS), PBGENE‑HBV from Precision Biosciences Inc. (NASDAQ:DTIL), and epigenetic silencers from [Tune Therapeutics Inc.](https://profiles.biocentury.com/companies/tune_therapeutics_inc) and nChroma Bio Inc. have all converged on chronic HBV as a proving ground, positioning the disease as a key proof-of-concept indication for next-generation nucleic acid and genome editing platforms...

title: ParcelBio: Engineering potent and durable mRNA for in vivo CAR Ts
raw_markdown:
ARTICLE \|Emerging Company Profile

# ParcelBio: Engineering potent and durable mRNA for in vivo CAR Ts

## ParcelBio’s APEXm mRNA platform aims for deeper immune reset over both linear and circular RNA designs

By Lindsay Martin, Biopharma Analyst

July 8, 2026 9:03 PM EDT

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

ParcelBio is aiming to solve the durability problem for _in vivo_ RNA-based CAR Ts by engineering stabilized mRNA to drive longer-lasting, deeper B-cell depletion beyond the current capabilities of linear mRNA or circular RNA designs for autoimmune diseases.

A number of [companies](https://www.biocentury.com/article/659637/kelonia-data-advance-the-durability-narrative-around-in-vivo-car-ts-at-asco) are beginning to figure out how to design and dose in _vivo_ CAR Ts to generate response rates that rival or top _ex vivo_ CAR Ts, but there’s debate in the field over whether they will match _ex vivo_ durability....
